model_status: reproducible Elo Monte Carlo with FIFA Annexe C third-place assignment table, explicit upset mechanisms, and configurable scenario factors; not an official forecast
data_notes: Teams and groups are entered as group-position data in data/teams.csv.
Elo ratings are stored in data/teams.csv and can be replaced by editing the elo column.
Group fixtures follow the FIFA four-team group-position pattern encoded in data/group_stage.csv.
Round-of-32 direct pairings and allowed third-place group sets are encoded in data/round32_slots.csv.
FIFA Regulations Annexe C is encoded in data/annex_c_third_place_assignments.csv with 495 options and columns 1A, 1B, 1D, 1E, 1G, 1I, 1K, 1L.
The validator checks that Annexe C contains all C(12,8)=495 third-place group combinations and that every assignment satisfies the allowed Round-of-32 third-place slot constraints.

Group ranking implements points first; for teams tied on points it applies head-to-head points, head-to-head goal difference, head-to-head goals scored, then overall goal difference and overall goals scored. Team conduct is not simulated; if teams.csv has fifa_rank and --fallback-ranking=fifa_rank is used, FIFA ranking can be used as the final fallback. Otherwise Elo is used as the deterministic fallback.

data/team_factors.csv contains editable heuristic scenario priors for depth, climate adaptation, travel resilience, upset potential, and upset resilience. These are not validated measurements and should be replaced or sensitivity-tested for serious analysis.

The moderate uncertainty profile uses tournament-level team rating noise, heavy-tailed match-level shocks, Gamma-Poisson goal overdispersion, an Elo shrinkage factor, explicit underdog shock events, and a 50/50 blend in drawn knockout tie-breakers. Individual options can override all defaults.
